A & T

Together with their families

AnandandTiuli

Sunday, April 25, 2027

Golden Gate Club · San Francisco

How We Met

One month, one walk, one questionable first date

For two years, Anand had been living out of a backpack with no fixed address and a happy disregard for anything resembling a plan. Tiuli was doing the opposite: after years in consulting, hopping cities and living out of suitcases, she had finally put down roots in San Francisco.

Then Anand swung through the city for exactly one month, timed to see his parents as they passed through from Chennai. One month, no big deal. Except it happened to be the same city, at the same time, as one Tiuli. They matched. They agreed to meet.

New to the city, he hunted for the perfect spot and found a coffee shop with a stunning view of the Golden Gate Bridge. He was very proud of himself. What he had not noticed was that the place was attached to the back of a rather seedy hostel.

Tiuli arrived, took one look around, and was understandably a little frightened. She stayed anyway, which, if you ask Anand, is the moment he knew she was extraordinary. What followed was six hours of conversation and the slow realization that two people who had lived such different lives had somehow orbited the same places. They wandered through Fort Mason with the Golden Gate glowing in the distance. Some people spend years searching. They needed one month, one walk, and one questionable recommendation.

Gallery

Moments along the way

Things To Do

Making a weekend of it in San Francisco

Brunch

Brunch at Piccino Presidio

1 Letterman Drive, Building D, San Francisco, CA 94129

Rated a top 20 SF brunch spot by The Infatuation. If you’re heading out for a morning stroll through the Presidio, make Piccino your first stop. It might not be your first SF farm-to-table restaurant, but it may just become your favorite. Floor-to-ceiling windows flood the space with light, and the relaxed, airy room makes it easy to settle in and stay a while. The strawberry-chantilly french toast is a must-order — but yes, always get the pizza with an egg on top, and the spring green garlic with anchovy and chili oil is the move.

There’s usually two-hour street parking in the area. The block of Lyon Street that runs parallel to the park, directly outside the restaurant, generally offers two-hour spaces.

View

Stroll Along the Presidio Tunnel Tops & Golden Gate Views

210 Lincoln Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94129

Just a 5 minute walk from our reception venue, the Golden Gate Club, Presidio Tunnel Tops offers some of the most panoramic and iconic views in all of San Francisco, overlooking the Golden Gate Bridge, Alcatraz, the Bay, and Crissy Field. You can spend the morning strolling through beautifully landscaped parks, relaxing in picnic areas throughout the grounds, and exploring the Presidio’s historic former Army homes and buildings that give the area its unique charm and character.

On the weekends, the park also hosts a solid lineup of rotating food trucks, making it an easy spot to grab lunch, coffee, or snacks before getting ready for the reception!

    Best Stops
  • Golden Gate Overlook
  • Immigrant Point Overlook
  • Food truck gathering area at the Main Lawn
  • Historic Officers’ homes and Presidio architecture walks
